input.initialPrompt:
I have a clean macOS machine and want to start developing .NET MAUI apps
for iOS and Android. I can't run commands right now, but I need a complete
setup plan. Tell me exactly what I need to install, how to discover the
right versions, and how to verify everything works. Please provide the
official recommended installation sources.

data.content:
Here's a complete setup plan for .NET MAUI development on a clean macOS machine:

---

## 1. Prerequisites

| Tool | Source | Purpose |
|------|--------|---------|
| **Xcode** | Mac App Store or [developer.apple.com](https://developer.apple.com/xcode/) | iOS SDK, simulators, build tools |
| **Xcode Command Line Tools** | `xcode-select --install` | CLI compilers & headers |
| **.NET SDK (latest LTS or STS)** | [dot.net/download](https://dot.net/download) | Runtime + CLI |
| **JDK 17** | Installed automatically by MAUI workload, or [Microsoft OpenJDK](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/java/openjdk/download) | Android build toolchain |
| **Android SDK** | Managed via .NET MAUI workload or [Android Studio](https://developer.android.com/studio) | Android platform APIs & emulator |

---

## 2. Installation Steps

```bash
# 1. Install Xcode from App Store, then accept license:
sudo xcodebuild -license accept

# 2. Install .NET SDK (use the installer from dot.net, or Homebrew):
brew install --cask dotnet-sdk

# 3. Install the MAUI workload (pulls Android SDK + JDK automatically):
sudo dotnet workload install maui

# 4. (Optional) Install Android emulator images:
#    The workload installs SDK; for emulators use Android Studio or:
dotnet android sdk install --package "system-images;android-34;google_apis;arm64-v8a"
```

---

## 3. Discover the Right Versions

```bash
# See installed .NET SDKs:
dotnet --list-sdks

# See available/installed workloads:
dotnet workload list
dotnet workload search maui

# Check which Android/iOS platforms are supported:
dotnet workload update   # keeps everything in sync

# Xcode version (must match Apple's required minimum for your iOS target):
xcodebuild -version
```

The [.NET MAUI release notes](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/dotnet/maui/whats-new/) document required minimum Xcode and Android SDK versions per release.

---

## 4. Verify the Installation

```bash
# Create and build a test app:
dotnet new maui -n HelloMaui
cd HelloMaui

# Build for iOS (requires Xcode):
dotnet build -f net9.0-ios

# Build for Android:
dotnet build -f net9.0-android

# Run on iOS Simulator:
dotnet build -f net9.0-ios -t:Run -p:_DeviceName=:v2:udid=<SIMULATOR_UDID>

# Run on Android Emulator (start emulator first):
dotnet build -f net9.0-android -t:Run
```

If both builds succeed, your environment is ready.

---

## 5. Optional but Recommended

- **Visual Studio Code** + [C# Dev Kit](https://marketplace.visualstudio.com/items?itemName=ms-dotnettools.csdevkit) — lightweight editor with MAUI support.
- **Visual Studio for Mac replacement** — VS for Mac is retired; VS Code is now the official path on macOS.
- **Android Studio** — only needed if you want a GUI for AVD/emulator management.
- **Hot Reload** — built in; just run with `dotnet watch` or from VS Code.

---

## Key Official References

- [.NET MAUI Installation Guide](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/dotnet/maui/get-started/installation)
- [.NET Download Page](https://dot.net/download)
- [Supported platforms matrix](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/dotnet/maui/supported-platforms)

The critical rule: **keep Xcode, .NET SDK, and the MAUI workload versions aligned** — `dotnet workload update` is the easiest way to stay current.
data.reasoningText: The user wants a complete setup plan for developing .NET MAUI apps on macOS targeting iOS and Android. They can't run commands right now, so they need a reference guide. Let me provide a comprehensive answer based on my knowledge of .NET MAUI development setup on macOS.
